mnemonics.title: Viewing Components
mnemonics.content: Remember VRP-VPN-VUP: VRP is the Point, VPN points the Way, VUP points Up - VRP = Reference Point, VPN = Normal direction, VUP = Upward direction

mnemonics.title: Clipping Planes
mnemonics.content: Front-Back-Top-Bottom-Left-Right = The 6 planes that 'clip' your 3D scene into a visible frustum shape

mnemonics.title: Pipeline Order
mnemonics.content: My Very Clever Professor Teaches Devices: Modeling → Viewing → Clipping → Projection → Transformation → Device coordinates

acronyms.usage: Origin of viewing coordinate system, like camera position
Vector perpendicular to view plane, defines look direction
Defines upward direction on view plane, prevents camera roll
Point from which projection rays emanate (perspective) or direction (parallel)
Intermediate coordinate system after projection and before viewport transform

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pyramid with the tip cut off - the tip is the camera/eye, the wide end is far away, the frustum captures everything visible
Perspective = railroad tracks converging to horizon; Parallel = architect's blueprint with lines staying truly parallel
Think of a magnifying glass (viewport) moving over a map (world) - what you see through the glass is what gets displayed
Point your finger (VPN) at what you want to see, then define up with your other hand (VUP) - that's your viewing orientation

analogies.analogy: Viewing transformation is like a movie camera: VRP is where you place the tripod, VPN is where you point the lens, VUP is how you hold the camera upright
The view window is like a rectangular window in a wall - you only see what's visible through that opening, everything else is hidden
The viewing frustum is like the beam of a flashlight - only objects within the cone of light get illuminated and rendered
